title: SPSS at UW-Madison
body: SPSS Statistics is a statistical software suite developed by IBM for data management, advanced analytics, multivariate analysis, and business intelligence. Eligibility Facstaff are eligible for SPSS access if they are in a University-paid position Students are eligible for SPSS access if they are currently enrolled as a student in a degree-seeking program at the University. For detailed questions about eligibility, see the CSL eligibility KB page: Software Available to Faculty/Staff and Students on Campus Software Library Facstaff access at UW-Madison SPSS can be accessed directly through the Campus Software Library after a valid NetID login. Student access at UW-Madison SPSS can be accessed directly through the Campus Software Library after a valid NetID login. What's included in SPSS for UW-Madison UW-Madison is licensed for the SPSS Statistics Premium software package.  A full feature list can be found here: Campus Edition Comparison How to get started with SPSS SPSS has interactive demos and tutorials available on their website, SPSS Resources Licensing Models UW-Madison is licensed for both individual installations and concurrent licenses for lab environments. Troubleshooting Common licensing errors can be found in Common SPSS Software Error Codes The most common error is not installing the software with administrator privileges.  Please contact your local IT for support with this error. Software questions and information on how to use the software can be found through the vendor, IBM SPSS Support and KB Additional help If you have more questions, please Get Help from DoIT .
